headline: Analysts say it's too early to call a top on semiconductor stocks
detail: Highly regarded analysts are pushing back on top-calling in semis, which directly supports SMH, SOXL, CRWD adjacent sentiment, and your overall tech-heavy book. This extends the narrative that the semi cycle has more room. For a margin trader, this is encouraging but not a green light to oversize.
rec: No specific trade triggered - you already own SMH and SOXL. Just confirms holding is right. Watch SMH price action; if it consolidates cleanly, existing positions are fine where they are.

headline: OpenAI/Anthropic backer MGX closes $49B AI fund
detail: One of the largest AI-focused capital raises ever signals institutional conviction in AI infrastructure spending - that means more demand for chips, cloud, and the names in your book. This is a slow-burn positive for NVDA, ALAB, and the semi complex broadly. Not a one-day pop catalyst but confirms the macro bid.
rec: NVDA has a scanner setup (entry 198.39-198.62, stop 197.35) but it scores only 7/12 with a grade C bias (4H is down). The AI fund news is real but not sharp enough today to justify a new leveraged buy. Watch NVDA for a cleaner setup.

headline: Tech leads first-half gains but biggest winners weren't U.S.
detail: U.S. tech led broadly but international names outperformed - this matters because it hints at rotation risk. Money chasing global tech could quietly reduce the flow into your concentrated U.S. chip names. Not an emergency, but worth noting as a positioning headwind.
rec: No action needed today. Just a flag that if QQQ or SMH start lagging global ETFs consistently, that rotation could be a trim signal for your broad index holdings.

headline: Oil wobbles as U.S.-Iran talks stall
detail: Geopolitical uncertainty in oil typically creates brief risk-off pulses. For your book it's indirect, but sharp oil moves can trigger broad sentiment selloffs that hit leveraged positions first and hardest. ERY (inverse energy 2x) actually has a scanner long setup today as a potential hedge angle.
rec: ERY scanner entry 12.93-12.95, stop 12.88 is a very tight, small setup. If you want a minor hedge against a risk-off oil spike, it's there - but the R is modest and it's not your core thesis. Strictly optional, small size only.
